task_completion.description: Agent created the 5 CSV condition files with 'stimulus', 'category', and 'corrAns' columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, spreadsheet application, or terminal output showing the contents of the CSV files with the required columns and stimuli (e.g., Rose, Ant, Joy, Agony).

task_completion.description: Experiment saved as iat_experiment.psyexp in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder window title bar displays 'iat_experiment.psyexp' or a save dialog shows the path '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/IAT/'.

task_completion.description: An instructions routine is placed at the start of the experiment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel at the bottom of the PsychoPy Builder shows an 'instructions' routine as the very first item in the timeline.

task_completion.description: Five block routines are added to the experiment flow.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel shows five distinct block routines (e.g., block1, block2, etc.) following the instructions routine.

task_completion.description: Loops are added around the block routines and linked to the CSV condition files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel shows loop indicators (circular arrows/brackets) around the block routines, OR a Loop Properties dialog is visible showing a path to a '.csv' file in the 'Conditions' field.

task_completion.description: Text components are created for the stimulus and the top-left/top-right category labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Routine panel shows multiple Text components (e.g., for the central stimulus and the corner labels), or a Text Component Properties dialog shows position settings like top-left and top-right.

task_completion.description: A keyboard component is added to record 'e' and 'i' responses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Routine panel shows a Keyboard component, or its Properties dialog shows 'e' and 'i' (or 'e', 'i') in the 'Allowed keys' field.

integrity.description: Agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to create the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding routines, components, editing properties), rather than just writing a .psyexp XML/JSON file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent created the CSV files manually or via standard commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing or formatting the CSV files in an editor (like nano, gedit, or LibreOffice Calc) or using echo/cat commands, rather than downloading a pre-made solution.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a python script to bypass the PsychoPy Builder GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal commands executing a python script that directly generates the .psyexp file without GUI interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/IAT/iat_experiment.psyexp. The required stimuli are exactly: Flowers (Rose, Tulip, Daisy, Lily), Insects (Ant, Spider, Beetle, Mosquito), Pleasant (Joy, Love, Peace, Happy), and Unpleasant (Agony, Terrible, Horrible, Nasty). The conditions files must be located in /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/IAT/conditions/.
